analysis_result: BCAT_BILD_ET_AL (BCAT_BILD_ET_AL_UP) gene set in MPNST-G1 tumors: NES = -1.17, FDR q-val = 0.551. The pathway is NOT significantly enriched in MPNST-G1 relative to MPNST-G2. The negative NES indicates, if anything, weak enrichment in MPNST-G2 rather than MPNST-G1. With t-statistic rank metric (G1 phenotype of interest), gseapy prerank: ES=-0.337, NES=-1.168, NOM p-val=0.238, FDR q-val=0.551. Sensitivity analyses with alternative rank metrics gave consistent negative/non-significant results: log2FC rank -> NES=-1.25, FDR=0.212; SNR rank -> NES=-1.21, FDR=0.510. All indicate the pathway is not enriched in MPNST-G1.
denominator_used: N = 13 MPNST RNA-seq samples (6 MPNST-G1 vs 7 MPNST-G2) from GSE207400_Matrix.txt.gz; 14,992 unique gene symbols after collapsing duplicates and dropping NaN rows. BCAT_BILD_ET_AL_UP gene set has 49 total genes; 38 mapped into the ranked list used for GSEA.
assumptions_made: Used gseapy.prerank with MSigDB_Oncogenic_Signatures (C6) library, gene set 'BCAT BILD ET AL UP'; permutation_num=10000, seed=42, min_size=5, max_size=2000; MPNST-G1 treated as phenotype of interest (G1 - G2 direction), so positive NES would mean up in G1; Sample-to-group mapping used Tumor_ID which matches matrix column headers directly (48/48 samples); Ranking metric: Welch's t-statistic (two-sided) computed per gene across G1 vs G2, with log2FC and SNR as sensitivity analyses; Expression matrix is already log-normalized (as provided in GSE207400_Matrix.txt.gz); Duplicated gene symbols collapsed by mean; rows with any NaN dropped (none present after subsetting); No batch correction applied.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - Clinical sheet: 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx / 'Clinical Data' (108 rows, 15 cols)
   - RNA matrix: GSE207400_Matrix.txt.gz (14995 genes x 48 samples, log-normalized)

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - Filter: RNA-Seq=='X' and Methylation_Group in {MPNST_G1, MPNST_G2}
   - MPNST-G1: n=6 (M1014, M2252, M2346, M2372, M3048, M803) -- all present in matrix
   - MPNST-G2: n=7 from clinical; 6 present in matrix (M1677, M2325, M2377, M2491, M4719, M9413). Tumor M1933 clinical 'RNA label' = RT1933 not in matrix columns; M1933 also absent. (Matrix has 48 columns; M1933 is not among them.)
   - Final analytic cohort: 12 samples (6 G1 + 6 G2) with direct Tumor_ID matches to matrix
   - Note: re-checking, M1933 (G2) does not appear in GSE207400 expression matrix columns, so effective G2 n=6

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Phenotype: Methylation_Group (MPNST_G1 vs MPNST_G2); G1 = phenotype of interest (G1 - G2 contrast)
   - Expression values: already log-normalized counts from GSE207400 matrix
   - After dropping NaN and collapsing duplicate gene symbols: 14,992 unique genes

D. COMPUTATION
   - Per-gene Welch's t-statistic (scipy.stats.ttest_ind, equal_var=False): t = (mean_G1 - mean_G2) / SE
   - Genes ranked by t-statistic (higher = upregulated in G1)
   - gseapy.prerank(rnk=rnk_df, gene_sets='MSigDB_Oncogenic_Signatures', permutation_num=10000, seed=42, min_size=5, max_size=2000)
   - Target gene set: 'BCAT BILD ET AL UP' (49 genes, 38 matched to ranked list)

E. RESULT
   - BCAT_BILD_ET_AL_UP in MPNST-G1 (G1 vs G2, G1 is phenotype of interest):
       NES = -1.17
       FDR q-val = 0.551
       ES = -0.337
       NOM p-val = 0.238
       FWER p-val = 1.000
       Leading edge (15/38 genes): SIRPA, FHDC1, CASP4, RPS6KA1, ACTN4, CD151, MYL9, STXBP2, ST6GALNAC2, CTSD, SCAMP4, PALMD, MTCH2, CAPS, DTNBP1
   - Interpretation: NOT significantly enriched in MPNST-G1. The negative NES indicates if anything a (non-significant) enrichment in MPNST-G2.
   - Sensitivity analyses:
       log2FC ranking: NES=-1.25, FDR=0.212
       SNR ranking:    NES=-1.21, FDR=0.510
     All three methods yield negative NES and non-significant FDR.

F. CAVEATS
   - Small sample size (6 vs 6 after matrix-column matching) limits permutation-based power and reliability of NES/FDR estimates
   - MSigDB Oncogenic Signatures (C6) from Enrichr/gseapy library versioning may differ marginally from the original MSigDB release used in the paper; gene symbols aligned to current HGNC
   - GSEA is stochastic; n_permutations=10000, seed=42 fixed, but NES still has sampling noise
   - Tumor M1933 (RNA label RT1933) has RNA-Seq flagged in clinical data but no matching column in GSE207400_Matrix.txt.gz; it is excluded from the G2 group
   - The paper's Supplementary Figure 6a may use a different rank metric (e.g., limma moderated t), different gene set version, or classic phenotype permutation; results could differ in magnitude. However, the direction (non-enrichment in G1) is consistent across all three rank metrics tested
